company_type: Agricultural, Construction and Forestry Equipment Manufacturing

regions: Central Europe and Commonwealth of Independent States (CIS)
Asia, Africa, Oceania, and Middle East

supply_chain.dependencies: Various raw materials including steel products, metal castings, forgings, plastics, hydraulics, electronics, and ready-to-assemble components from leading suppliers domestically and internationally. Some materials and components are acquired from a single supplier or are proprietary in nature and cannot be alternatively sourced expeditiously.
supply_chain.risks: Changes in availability and price of raw materials, components, and whole goods have resulted in disruptions to supply chain causing production disruptions, increased costs, and lower profits. Global logistics network challenges resulted in delays, shortages of key manufacturing components, increased order backlogs, increased transportation costs, and production inefficiencies. Work interruption or union strikes by employees of suppliers could contribute to disruptions. Geopolitical tensions including Russia/Ukraine war and Middle East conflict have affected supply chain.

regulatory_risks: U.S., European Union, India, and other governments' laws and regulations to reduce off-road engine emissions requiring significant investments in new engine technologies and after-treatment systems

Laws regulating products across their life cycles including raw material sourcing, storage, distribution, sale, use, and disposal (right-to-know, restriction of hazardous substances, product take-back laws)

Complex and changing international, federal, state, and local laws including advertising, anti-money laundering, antitrust, consumer finance, environmental, climate-related, health and safety, foreign exchange controls, foreign ownership and investment, import/export and trade, human rights, labor and employment, product liability reporting, cybersecurity, data privacy, telematics, encryption, and telecommunications regulations
U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) and foreign anti-bribery and anti-corruption laws
Changes in tax laws and regulations affecting foreign income, tax loss carryforwards, and deferred tax assets
Government policies on spending affecting infrastructure development
Policies impacting exchange rates and commodity prices, or limiting export/import of commodities
Changes in government farm programs and policies influencing demand for agricultural equipment
Governmental actions designed to address climate change and transition to lower-carbon economy
Expanding export controls or limits on foreign investment impacting global supply of key materials and components
Trade restrictions, negotiation of new trade agreements, non-tariff trade barriers, local content requirements, and imposition of new or retaliatory tariffs
Federal Trade Commission (FTC) investigation regarding repair of John Deere brand agricultural equipment and information security practices
Regulations relating to climate and environmental risk for financial services segment
Right to repair or right to modify regulations and legislation
New or retaliatory tariffs against certain countries or covering certain products

sanction_risks: Changing U.S. export controls and sanctions on various foreign countries affecting ability to collect receivables, provide aftermarket warranty support, and sell products
Russian government restrictions on companies' abilities to repatriate or remit cash from Russian-based operations
Suspension of shipments to Russia due to Russia/Ukraine war events
US-China trade conflict impacting business in China and sales/imports/exports with Chinese entities globally
Argentine government capital and currency controls restricting access to U.S. dollars and remitting earnings
